meta.name: Saketh Metta
meta.title: CS Researcher & Systems Engineer
meta.desc: MS Computer Science student at Oregon State University. I modify PostgreSQL internals, run a two-node k3s homelab, and build agentic AI automation that actually ships.

about.ta:
CS 325 — Analysis of Algorithms
CS 440 — Database Management
about.targets: AI/ML Eng · Systems Eng · Data Eng · Research SWE
about.interests: ML Systems · Robotics · Distributed Infra · Photography

projects.title: Agentic Job Application Pipeline
projects.body: An AI agent that discovers job postings, fills application forms, and drafts answers under strict no-fabrication rules — nothing is submitted without human sign-off. A headless Claude Code agent drives a persistent Chromium via the Playwright MCP server, n8n orchestrates scanning, dedup, and Discord approve/decline gates, and a local Llama 3.1 8B handles high-frequency field routing. Safety is structural: the agent's standing rules forbid clicking Submit, and only human approval unlocks it — validated against prompt injection in live runs. Every application leaves a full audit trail.

projects.title: Personal RAG Chatbot
projects.body: Full RAG pipeline running entirely on my homelab — no external APIs. Questions hit a FastAPI endpoint, ChromaDB retrieves matching context via nomic-embed-text embeddings, and Qwen 2.5:3B (served by Ollama on the GTX 1060) answers in first person. Runs as a container on k3s behind Cloudflare Tunnel, and powers the chat widget on this site.

projects.title: sakethmetta.org
projects.body: This portfolio — Node.js/Express in a container on k3s, exposed via Cloudflare Tunnel, with an AI chatbot backed by my self-hosted RAG pipeline. No managed hosting anywhere in the path.

projects.title: TA Grading Automation
projects.body: Python pipeline that unzips student submissions, compiles the C++ with g++, runs the binaries, validates output ordering, and writes a grade summary CSV — cutting hours of manual grading per assignment across CS 325 and CS 440.

projects.title: Job Search Tooling
projects.body: Config-driven, proxy-aware Python scraper targeting AI/ML, systems, and data roles. Surfaces high-signal postings from the job-board firehose and feeds the discovery stage of the agentic application pipeline.

projects.title: ROS2 Navigation Stack
projects.body: Working with ROS2 Jazzy on TurtleBot3 simulation and the Nav2 navigation stack in Gazebo. Parallel interest in ESP32/BLE room detection and embedded IoT integration.

projects.title: Jarvis-style Personal Assistant
projects.body: Long-horizon build: a local, voice-driven assistant for hands-free task automation, web search, data retrieval, and media playback — combining LLM inference, speech recognition, and system integration.

software.summary: Working alongside professional software engineers on the enterprise Salesforce platform behind Oregon State's academic operations — curriculum, scheduling, and degree tracking.

contributions.title: Curriculum Change Automation
contributions.body: Integrated DocuSign PDFs into Salesforce Flows so curriculum changes route and record automatically instead of by hand.

contributions.title: Course Validation
contributions.body: Added warnings in the program-of-study tool that catch zero-credit and duplicate courses before they corrupt degree tracking.

contributions.title: Grade Policy Enforcement
contributions.body: Customized approval workflows to enforce departmental grade requirements automatically.

contributions.title: Access & Reporting
contributions.body: Built custom permission sets for faculty committee assignments and exam scheduling.

contributions.title: Platform Work
contributions.body: Developed Apex classes and Lightning Web Components with cross-functional university teams.

unrelated.body: Managed and trained a team of 20 supporting Spotify's artist content operations; recognized as best advisor for 24+ months. Built out a direct feature-request service for artists and served as backup Real-Time Analyst, keeping the floor on service-level targets.
Troubleshot hardware and software issues for Amazon Echo and Kindle devices, trained new advisors on support processes, and held a top-performer record against metrics throughout tenure.
